consumer_projections.support_summary: Graph projection resolves every infrastructure object and relation.
Review projection reuses the shared object packet so live counterpart, applied-by, owned-by, impacts, and review anchors stay explicit.
Docs projection reuses the shared object packet so lineage, live counterpart, ownership, impact, runbook, and review cues stay in one vocabulary.
Incident projection reuses the shared object packet so live counterpart, applied-by, owned-by, impacts, runbooks, and overlays stay target-scoped.

source_object_packet.support_summary: Canonical infrastructure object packet keeps authored, rendered, planned, observed, and provider-overlay facts explicit.

relation_graph_selections.support_summary: Terraform incident/export parity reopens the same checkout relation graph.
Kubernetes parity keeps the stale rollout overlay explicit beside live cluster truth.
Devcontainer parity preserves remote-helper provenance and retry-required skew.
CI parity preserves the managed environment slice and its hosted overlay lineage.
Policy parity preserves permission-limited and unsupported-skew posture explicitly.

handoff_lineage.support_summary: Terraform export preserves the vendor-console lineage for the checkout service.

Devcontainer export preserves the workspace-container overlay handoff lineage.
CI export preserves the hosted environment handoff lineage and return anchor.
Policy export preserves the permission-gap handoff lineage to the provider dashboard.
